description: Envelope Encryption — auto-generate per-project encryption keys, wrap with PBKDF2-derived key from developer password, auto-decrypt in dashboard on login

userStories.title: Database migration — add wrapped_encryption_key column
userStories.description: As the system, I need a wrapped_encryption_key column on the projects table so that wrapped encryption key blobs can be stored per-project.
acceptanceCriteria: Alembic migration adds nullable bytea column wrapped_encryption_key to projects
Project SQLAlchemy model includes wrapped_encryption_key: Mapped[bytes | None]
Existing projects have NULL for this column (backward compatible)
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds

userStories.title: Dashboard envelope crypto module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need client-side crypto functions (PBKDF2 key derivation, AES-256-GCM wrap/unwrap, random key generation) so the dashboard can manage envelope encryption entirely in the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: New module dashboard/src/lib/envelope-crypto.ts with deriveWrappingKey, generateEncryptionKey, wrapKey, unwrapKey
deriveWrappingKey uses PBKDF2-SHA256 with 600K iterations and salt pqdb-envelope-v1:email
generateEncryptionKey generates random 32 bytes as base64url string
wrapKey/unwrapKey use AES-256-GCM with 12-byte nonce prefix
All crypto uses WebCrypto API (crypto.subtle) — no new dependencies
Round-trip test: generate → wrap → unwrap → verify equality
Wrong password fails unwrap (throws DOMException)
Different emails produce different wrapping keys
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
userStories.notes: PR #99 merged. envelope-crypto.ts with 7 unit tests.

userStories.title: Backend API — store and return wrapped encryption key
userStories.description: As the dashboard, I need backend endpoints to store and retrieve the wrapped encryption key blob so it persists across sessions and devices.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /v1/projects accepts optional wrapped_encryption_key (base64 string)
GET /v1/projects and GET /v1/projects/{id} return wrapped_encryption_key (base64 string or null)
New endpoint PATCH /v1/projects/{id}/encryption-key updates the wrapped blob (requires developer JWT)
New endpoint POST /v1/auth/change-password accepts current_password and new_password, verifies old password, updates hash, returns new token pair
Base64 encoding/decoding handled at API boundary (bytea in DB, base64 string in JSON)
Integration tests: create project with wrapped key, verify returned in GET
Integration tests: PATCH updates blob correctly
Integration tests: change-password verifies old password, rejects wrong password
Unit tests pass
Integration tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Service responds to health check
userStories.notes: PR #100 merged. API endpoints + change-password + base64 validation fix.

userStories.title: Dashboard — auto-decrypt on login
userStories.description: As a developer logging in with a password, I want encrypted columns to automatically decrypt without needing to enter a separate encryption key.
acceptanceCriteria: New EnvelopeKeyProvider context at app root holds wrapping key + per-project encryption keys map
Login page runs PBKDF2 derivation in parallel with login API call (hides latency)
Signup page does the same (wrapping key ready for first project creation)
After login, when projects are loaded, wrapped blobs are auto-unwrapped
Projects with no wrapped_encryption_key (e.g. created via MCP server) auto-generate a key, wrap it, and PATCH to server when wrapping key is available — no user prompt needed
Table detail pages auto-call unlock(key) on EncryptionProvider when envelope key is available
Existing EncryptionProvider / useEncryption() API unchanged (backward compatible)
Manual Unlock dialog remains functional as fallback (OAuth/passkey users)
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser: login → navigate to table with encrypted columns → columns auto-decrypt
userStories.notes: PR #101 merged. EnvelopeKeyProvider, parallel PBKDF2 login, auto-unlock, error resilience.

userStories.title: Dashboard — generate and wrap key on project creation
userStories.description: As a developer creating a project, I want an encryption key to be automatically generated and wrapped so I don't need to manage keys manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Project creation dialog generates a random encryption key via generateEncryptionKey()
Key is wrapped with the wrapping key from EnvelopeKeyProvider context
Wrapped blob is sent as wrapped_encryption_key in POST /v1/projects
Unwrapped key is stored in EnvelopeKeyProvider.encryptionKeys map
If no wrapping key is available (OAuth login), project is created without wrapped key
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: PR #103 merged. Auto-gen + wrap on project creation, OAuth fallback.

userStories.title: Dashboard — reveal encryption key in project settings
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to reveal and copy my project's encryption key from the settings page so I can use it with the SDK.
acceptanceCriteria: Project settings page shows Encryption Key section
Reveal button displays the key from EnvelopeKeyProvider (already unwrapped in memory)
Copy button copies key to clipboard
Hide button re-hides the key
Warning text: key is never stored by pqdb, store it securely, cannot be recovered if lost
If no encryption key available (OAuth login, no wrapped blob), shows explanation message
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: PR #102 merged. RevealEncryptionKey component with Reveal/Copy/Hide.

userStories.title: Password change with re-wrapping
userStories.description: As a developer changing my password, I want all my project encryption keys to be re-wrapped with the new password so auto-decrypt continues working.
acceptanceCriteria: Change Password UI in account settings (dialog with current + new password fields)
On submit: call POST /v1/auth/change-password, then re-wrap all project keys client-side
Re-wrapping: unwrap each blob with old wrapping key, wrap with new wrapping key, PATCH each project
Update wrapping key in EnvelopeKeyProvider
No re-encryption of actual data needed (only the envelope changes)
If re-wrapping fails for a project, skip it and warn (don't block password change)
Unit tests pass
Integration tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: PR #104 merged. Change password dialog with re-wrapping, partial failure resilience.

userStories.title: MCP server — auto-receive encryption key via OAuth callback
userStories.description: As a developer connecting to the MCP server via OAuth, I want the encryption key to be passed automatically during the OAuth callback so I don't need to manually copy it from the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard mcp-callback.ts includes the unwrapped encryption key in the OAuth redirect URL alongside the JWT token
Dashboard derives wrapping key from password during MCP OAuth login, unwraps project encryption key, adds as encryption_key query param
If no wrapping key or no wrapped encryption key available, encryption_key param is omitted (graceful fallback)
MCP server oauth-provider.ts extracts encryption_key from the callback and stores it in config
MCP server http-app.ts passes the encryption key to createPqdbMcpServer when creating the SDK client
PQDB_ENCRYPTION_KEY env var still works as override — if set, it takes precedence over the OAuth-provided key
MCP CRUD tools automatically decrypt encrypted columns when the key is available via OAuth
Unit tests: dashboard mcp-callback includes encryption key when available
Unit tests: MCP oauth-provider extracts and stores encryption key
Unit tests: MCP falls back gracefully when no encryption key in callback
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
userStories.notes: PR #105 merged. Dashboard passes encryption key in MCP OAuth callback. MCP server extracts and uses it.
